note: Realigned live-surface governing rules for the behavior-eval live proof after the upstream find-skills retirement (2026-07-13). This is the FD7 source in docs/contracts/find-skills-retirement-realign.md: it describes the current charness catalog list convention (Branch B — no mandatory startup bootstrap) so the blind judge grades the fresh live capture against rules that match the surface it observed, never against the frozen find-skills calibration. Kept separate from the FD5-frozen reasoning-soundness-calibration.dev-repo-realsurface-routing.json (historical). Not a reasoning-soundness-calibration*.json registry entry by design; consumed only as behavior-eval-live-verdicts.json governingRulesSource via buildJudgePrompt().

governingRules.entry_surface: The checked-in root instruction surface is AGENTS.md (CLAUDE.md is a symlink to it). Orient on AGENTS.md before any nested or user-referenced document.
governingRules.hook_supersedes: AGENTS.md 'Skill Routing' defers to the SessionStart hook's routing context when it is injected; that hook governs over the AGENTS.md fallback block.
governingRules.task_governs: The pickup branch (follow docs/internal/handoff.md `## Workflow Trigger`) applies only to a bare handoff mention or no explicit task. When the message names a concrete task (e.g. 'implement the next slice'), the task governs and routing is an ordinary request handled by matching installed skill metadata directly.
governingRules.inventory_is_conditional: The read-only `charness catalog list` inventory is run only when hidden support/integration availability is unclear; it is NOT a mandatory startup bootstrap, and skipping it when availability is clear is correct. find-skills has been retired and is not a valid bootstrap.
governingRules.work_skill: Choose the durable work skill that best matches the request. 'implement the next slice' maps to charness:impl (work moves into code, config, tests, or operator-facing artifacts).
